LanguageData.AmbrosiaAddiction.description: A chemical addiction to punga fruit. Long-term use has caused neurological changes, making it hard to function without the fruit. Withdrawal is tough, but with time, the body can recover.
LanguageData.AmbrosiaAddiction.label: punga addiction
LanguageData.AmbrosiaHigh.description: Punga chemicals in the bloodstream. It lifts the mood and helps survivors forget the harshness of the wasteland.
LanguageData.AmbrosiaHigh.label: punga warmth
LanguageData.AmbrosiaTolerance.description: A built-up tolerance to punga fruit. The more severe this tolerance is, the more punga it takes to get the same effect.
LanguageData.AmbrosiaTolerance.label: punga tolerance
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyAsIngredient.description: This colonist drank an atomic cocktail that had pungashine as one of its ingredients.
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyAsIngredient.label: drank pungashine-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_AmbrandyInspiration.label: pungashine inspiration
LanguageData.VBE_ConsumedCocktail.description: Consumed a nice atomic cocktail. Has a chance to provide a random inspiration.
LanguageData.VBE_ConsumedCocktail.label: consumed atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_DrankSoda.description: A burst of sugar from a bottle of homebrew sarsaparilla provides a short-lived lift. Provides a 5% manipulation buff for 5 hours.
LanguageData.VBE_DrankSoda.label: drank homebrew sarsaparilla
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrinkCrash.description: An unpleasant delayed after-effect of guarana consumption.
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrinkCrash.label: guarana crash
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrinkHigh.description: Guarana restores rest immediately and increases movement speed, but it always leaves a crash behind it.
LanguageData.VBE_EnergyDrinkHigh.label: guarana high
LanguageData.VBE_GinAsIngredient.label: drank gin-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_RBMeadAsIngredient.label: drank mead-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_TequilaAsIngredient.label: drank tequila-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_VodkaAsIngredient.label: drank vodka-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_WhiskeyAsIngredient.label: drank whiskey-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VBE_WineAsIngredient.label: drank wine-based atomic cocktail
LanguageData.VCE_ConsumedDigestibleResurrectorNanites: A dose of aqua cura is active in this person's bloodstream. If they die while it remains active, the suspended repair nanites will try to rebuild them on the spot.
LanguageData.VCE_ConsumedSoySauce.description: A tangy cranberry sauce added depth and sweetness to this meal.
LanguageData.VCE_ConsumedSoySauce.label: consumed cranberry sauce (from meal)
LanguageData.VCE_ConsumedWasabi.label: consumed xander root (from meal)
LanguageData.GravNausea.description: Intense nausea caused by sudden acceleration or violent deceleration aboard the Hellion.
LanguageData.GravNausea.label: flight sickness
LanguageData.RobCo_EyebotTargetingUplink.description: Receives targeting data from a nearby Eyebot.
LanguageData.RobCo_EyebotTargetingUplink.label: eyebot targeting uplink
LanguageData.RobCo_WarMachineLiberatorUplink.descript: Receives a combat uplink from a nearby War Machine.
LanguageData.RobCo_WarMachineLiberatorUplink.label: war machine uplink
LanguageData.Chemshined.label: rotgut
LanguageData.DeathrestExhaustion.description: Severe exhaustion caused by prolonged Wendigo Shakes.
LanguageData.DeathrestExhaustion.label: wendigo shakes exhaustion
LanguageData.GlucosoidRush.description: Metabolic activity is temporarily increased to fight the Wendigo Shakes.
LanguageData.GlucosoidRush.label: glucosoid rush
LanguageData.HemogenAmplified.description: Blood production is temporarily boosted to counteract the effects of the Wendigo Shakes.
LanguageData.HemogenAmplified.label: hemogen boost
LanguageData.InterruptedDeathrest.description: Recovery from the Wendigo Shakes was interrupted, worsening symptoms.
LanguageData.InterruptedDeathrest.label: interrupted recovery
LanguageData.PsychofluidRush.description: Mental stability is temporarily increased to resist the Wendigo Shakes.
LanguageData.PsychofluidRush.label: psychofluid rush
LanguageData.WestTek_FEVMutationPending_1.description: An unrefined FEV dosage is tearing through this subject's body in violent, uneven waves. Their metabolism surges, tissues misfire, and the mind slips toward paranoia and collapse while the mutation struggles to take hold.
LanguageData.WestTek_FEVMutationPending_1.label: unstable FEV exposure
LanguageData.WestTek_FEVMutationPending_2.description: A refined FEV strain is restructuring this subject with far more control, but the process is still brutal. Violent mood swings, intrusive aggression, and bouts of instability are common until the body either stabilizes or fails.
LanguageData.WestTek_FEVMutationPending_2.label: refined FEV exposure
LanguageData.WestTek_FEVMutationPending_3.description: An isolated FEV culture is remaking this subject at the deepest biological level. The transformation is faster and cleaner than lesser strains, but the nervous system remains under immense pressure until the final mutation locks into place.
LanguageData.WestTek_FEVMutationPending_3.label: isolated FEV exposure
LanguageData.VFED_Invisibility.description: A stealthboy cloak granted by the Stealthboy device. It affects nearby observers, making their brains unable to perceive this individual even if their eyes remain unaffected.
LanguageData.VFED_Invisibility.label: stealthboy cloak
LanguageData.GutWorms.label: rad worms
LanguageData.GutWorms.description: Parasitic worms found in irradiated soil and contaminated food throughout the wasteland. They consume nutrients in the gut alongside the host, causing severe malnutrition even in those who eat regularly. Common in settlements with unfiltered water or improperly preserved food.
LanguageData.FibrousMechanites.label: cloud pustules
LanguageData.FibrousMechanites.description: A skin irritation acquired through exposure to a mysterious cloud fallout. The residue forms dense fibrous deposits throughout connective tissue and produce visible subcutaneous pustules. The infestation temporarily enhances physical capacity but causes worsening pain as the load increases.
LanguageData.SensoryMechanites.label: cloud lung
LanguageData.SensoryMechanites.description: A respiratory infestation acquired through inhaling mysterious cloud fallout. The residue lodges in the respiratory tract and nervous system, initially sharpening the senses before progressively overloading neural pathways. Worsening pain and sensory interference are the hallmarks of an advancing infection.
LanguageData.Plague.label: new plague
LanguageData.Plague.description: A viral infection endemic across pre-War America. Spreads rapidly once established, overwhelming the immune system within days. Without treatment, most infected individuals die. Standard medicine can suppress the virus, while advanced medical protocols can eliminate the infection entirely.
LanguageData.Malaria.label: bloatfly fever
LanguageData.Malaria.description: A parasitic infection transmitted by bloatfly bites. The parasites incubate in the bloodstream for days before symptoms appear. Bloatfly fever is rarely lethal on its own, but causes recurring fever episodes that significantly reduce the victim's capacity for work. Common in low-lying areas where bloatfly populations are dense.
LanguageData.SleepingSickness.label: bayou fungus
LanguageData.SleepingSickness.description: A slow-spreading fungal infection originating in radioactive stagnant water. The spores progressively saturate neural tissue, causing deepening fatigue and diminished cognitive function. Recovery is slow. Common near stagnant water and dense organic decay.
LanguageData.ScariaInfection.label: scorched plague
LanguageData.ScariaInfection.description: A bacterial infection derived from the Scorched Plague pathogen. The bacteria erode self-control progressively as they reach saturation. Without treatment, the host will eventually descend into a berserk Scorched state. The infection can be halted at any stage with standard medicine.
LanguageData.Abasia.label: shell shock
LanguageData.Abasia.description:
Shell shock is a severe psychosomatic breakdown triggered by exposure to the unrelenting violence of modern combat. The nervous system, overwhelmed by the sensory assault of weapons fire, explosions, and close-quarters killing, shuts down voluntary motor function as a defensive response. The patient is physically unharmed but cannot walk — the commands the brain sends to the limbs no longer arrive.
Recovery occurs naturally as the mind and neural pathways slowly repair, though the process takes time. A specialized surgical procedure can restore motor function immediately by reconstructing the broken pathways.
LanguageData.Abasia.descriptionShort: A psychosomatic breakdown caused by extreme combat trauma, leaving the patient unable to walk despite no physical injury.
